phase1.output: (Window with buttons that open various types of dialogs and alerts)
phase1.explanation:
Dialogs and alerts provide user interaction:

Alert Types:
• INFORMATION — informational message
• WARNING — warning message
• ERROR — error message
• CONFIRMATION — yes/no/cancel
• NONE — custom buttons

Alert methods:
• setTitle() — window title
• setHeaderText() — bold header text
• setContentText() — main message
• showAndWait() — show and wait for response

Dialog Types:

TextInputDialog:
• Asks for text input
• getValue() returns entered text
• Optional<String> result

ChoiceDialog:
• Choose from a list of options
• First argument is default
• Optional<String> result

Custom Dialog:
• Build your own dialog
• Add any controls
• Set result converter

ButtonType:
• OK, CANCEL, YES, NO, CLOSE, APPLY
• ButtonType.OK, ButtonType.CANCEL, etc.
• Custom: new ButtonType("Custom")

Optional:
• Use Optional for safe handling
• ifPresent() for when value exists
• orElse() for default value

Common patterns:
• Confirmation dialogs before destructive actions
• Input dialogs for user data
• Custom dialogs for complex forms

⚠️ Dialogs must be shown on the JavaFX Application Thread

phase2.errorMessage: (Window appears but the alert is NEVER SHOWN!)
phase2.explanation:
You created the alert but never called show() or showAndWait()!

FIX: Add:
alert.show();  // Non-blocking

or
alert.showAndWait();  // Blocking (waits for response)

Always call show() or showAndWait() to display the dialog!

Common mistake: creating the alert but forgetting to display it.

phase3.prompt:
Write a program that:
1. Has a Button "Delete File"
2. When clicked, shows a CONFIRMATION alert with message "Are you sure you want to delete this file?"
3. If OK is clicked, prints "File deleted!" to a Label
4. If Cancel is clicked, prints "Deletion cancelled" to a Label
